body.title: Granny instant-wake via filesystem signal (restore sub-poll dispatch)
body.description:
Granny's daemon lost its instant wake when #5 (T-ticket-pg-drop) removed the Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY on devlab.tickets INSERT. It now waits out POLL_INTERVAL_S (default 60s) between cycles, so a newly-filed/freed ticket can sit up to ~60s before dispatch. (Note: this latency was already live pre-#5 — NOTIFY stopped firing the moment filing moved to the FS store, since nothing INSERTs to devlab.tickets anymore. #5 just made it explicit.) Restore near-instant wake with a FILESYSTEM signal so there is no Postgres dependency.

Proposed shape: have ticket_store.write/_put bump a well-known signal file's mtime (or write to a wake FIFO) on every ticket mutation; the daemon's _wait_for_work watches that mtime (cheap stat at a short sub-interval, or inotify via the existing watchdog dep if present) and preempts the sleep when it changes. No new hard dependency — a directory-mtime stat-poll is sufficient and matches the no-SQLite/flat-file discipline.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (_wait_for_work), unseen_university/ticket_store.py (emit wake signal on _put)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (flat-file signal only), external-state-principle
**Scope boundary:** IN: FS wake signal + daemon preempt. OUT: changing the poll interval default, any Postgres path (already removed).
**Completion criteria:** a ticket written to the store while the daemon is sleeping preempts the sleep and dispatches within ~1-2s (not the full poll interval), with Postgres stopped; covered by a daemon wake test.
**Test plan:** unit test that _wait_for_work returns early when the signal mtime/FIFO changes mid-sleep; integration check that a fresh sprint ticket dispatches sub-poll-interval.
